title: Lipidomic Analysis of Neuronal Lipid Droplets
author: Mukesh Kumar

steps.title: Prepare the LD samples for extraction...
steps.instruction: Prepare the LD samples for extraction by ensuring they are thawed On ice and homogenized.

steps.title: Add MTBE to the LD samples...
steps.instruction: Add MTBE to the LD samples along with spiked Avanti Splashmix (internal standards) to normalize lipid quantification.

steps.title: Vortex the mixture thoroughly to ensure complete mixing
steps.instruction: Vortex the mixture thoroughly to ensure complete mixing.

steps.title: Centrifuge the mixture to separate the organic phase
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the mixture to separate the organic phase.

steps.title: Collect the organic phase carefully to...
steps.instruction: Collect the organic phase carefully to avoid contamination from the aqueous phase.

steps.title: Dry the collected organic phase under...
steps.instruction: Dry the collected organic phase under a gentle stream of nitrogen or in a vacuum concentrator.

steps.title: Resuspend the dried lipid extract in 1-Butanol
steps.instruction: Resuspend the dried lipid extract in 1-Butanol.

steps.title: Vortex the sample to ensure uniform suspension
steps.instruction: Vortex the sample to ensure uniform suspension.

steps.title: Load 3 µL of the lipid...
steps.instruction: Load 3 µL of the lipid mixture onto the LC-MS system.

steps.title: Set up the Agilent Poroshell 120 EC-C18 column (2
steps.instruction: Set up the Agilent Poroshell 120 EC-C18 column (2.7 μm, 2.1 × 100 mm) on the Agilent 1290 Infinity II LC system.

steps.title: Prepare the following mobile phases
steps.instruction: Prepare the following mobile phases: Mobile Phase A (60% Acetonitrile, 40% H₂O, 7.5 mM Ammonium Acetate), Mobile Phase B (90% IPA, 10% Acetonitrile, 7.5 mM Ammonium Acetate).

steps.title: Use the following gradient for lipid separation
steps.instruction: Use the following gradient for lipid separation: Start at 85% A (15% B) and decrease to 70% A over 00:02:00 . Further decrease to 52% A over 00:00:30 . Gradually reduce to 18% A over 00:05:00 . Reduce to 1% A within 00:01:00 and hold for 00:04:00 .

steps.title: Restore gradient to 85% A and...
steps.instruction: Restore gradient to 85% A and wash the column for 00:05:00 .

steps.title: Perform the analysis on the Agilent...
steps.instruction: Perform the analysis on the Agilent Quadrupole Time-Of-Flight 6546 mass spectrometer for high-resolution detection of lipid species.

steps.title: Process the LC-MS data using MS-DIAL software (version 4
steps.instruction: Process the LC-MS data using MS-DIAL software (version 4.7; PMID: 25938372).

steps.title: Identify lipid species based on the...
steps.instruction: Identify lipid species based on the mass spectra and retention times.

steps.title: Quantify each lipid species using the...
steps.instruction: Quantify each lipid species using the moles of corresponding class-specific lipid standards present in the Splashmix. •Ensure the organic phase is free from aqueous contamination to avoid interference in LC-MS. •Handle MTBE and other organic solvents with care in a fume hood to prevent exposure. •The gradient setup must be precise to ensure reproducible lipid separation and identification.
